measurements.measurement_reference: Ground floor window and door openings used for vertical scale. Estimated 18 courses per 1.3m window height.

brick_analysis.bond_notes: Standard half-lap running bond for main field. The diaper (diamond) pattern is achieved by inserting contrasting-colour headers at regular intervals within the running bond field, creating a diamond motif without changing the bond pattern itself.

colour.description: Medium red-brown brick as main field colour with contrasting darker and/or buff brick used for the diamond/diaper pattern. The contrast creates a visible decorative motif against the red field. Some natural variation in the red field brick.
colour.polychromatic_notes: Diaper (diamond) pattern formed by contrasting brick headers inserted at regular intervals in the running bond field. The diamonds appear as a repeating geometric motif on the first-floor facade between windows. This is a distinctive decorative technique common in late Victorian Toronto residential architecture (1880s-1890s).

special_features.location: first floor facade, between and below windows
special_features.description: Diamond/diaper pattern formed by contrasting-colour brick headers set into the running bond field. Pattern creates repeating diamond shapes approximately 8-10 courses tall. A distinctive and relatively uncommon decorative feature in Kensington Market.
Flat jack arches above window openings
Brick rowlock course forming window sills

condition.notes: Overcast winter photo with snow on ground. Building appears to be a 2-storey residential brick house. The diaper pattern is the most notable feature — survives intact and is an important heritage detail for 3D model accuracy. Some weathering and soiling but no major deterioration visible. White van partially obscures ground floor in photo.

texture_calibration.notes: Photo taken at slight oblique angle in overcast winter conditions. Snow and van partially obscure lower facade. The diaper pattern provides additional geometric reference for calibration — diamond repeat spacing can cross-check course height estimates.
